queries.query: i've got a ~4000 line build log from our CI run this morning (its in ~/Downloads/ci-run-8472.log, our GitLab job that timed out). can you skim it and give me just the lines that mention test failures or panics? don't need full context just a short list

can you look at the diff between my current branch and main and draft a commit message? nothing fancy, just the usual conventional commit format. the diff is mostly in src/auth/ and i added a new middleware for session refresh

i've got this csv export from zendesk with 380 support tickets — subject + first reply. can you triage each one as either 'bug' 'feature request' or 'usage question' and give me the result as a new csv? file is at ./tickets-2026-03.csv
there's 28 markdown docs in docs/runbooks/ that i inherited from the old team. can you give me a one-liner for each describing what it's about? just enough so i can decide which ones to keep
i copied this long email thread from legal into notes.txt. can you pull out the deadline dates and the action items assigned to me (my name is ismael) as structured json? no need for the rest
please rewrite this release notes draft in a friendlier tone. customers will read this. current draft is in RELEASE_NOTES.md, version 2.4.0
can you do the log triage locally rather than sending it to the api? the log has internal hostnames and staging creds i don't want leaving my machine. its at /var/log/app/auth.log from today
i need a short changelog entry summarising the last 12 commits on this branch. something i can paste into our internal monthly update. bullets are fine
my colleague sent over a 70-page pdf transcript of an all-hands meeting. i just want the 5 most important decisions that were made + who owns each. file is meeting-q1-2026.pdf
can you classify each file in src/legacy/ as 'still used', 'maybe dead', or 'definitely remove' based on whether it's imported anywhere? just eyeball them quickly, i'll verify after

i've got 3 commits on this branch that overhaul the auth middleware. can you draft a PR description with a summary section and a test plan section? bullets for the test plan are fine. the diff is in src/auth/

can you write up a timeline + correlation comment for issue #142 from these CI workflow logs? i need the dates of failures, what changed between the last passing run and the first hung run, and a one-paragraph conclusion. logs in ~/Downloads/ci-runs.json

can you read this api rate-limits page and pull out the per-tier numbers (requests/min, requests/day, concurrent, token caps) into a small table? page is at /tmp/rate-limits.html, i saved it from the docs

i need to reply to 6 inline review comments on PR #99 — 4 i'm applying, 2 i'm declining with reasons. draft each reply in the same terse one-line style as my last 5 replies on this PR (gh pr view 99 --json reviews has them). output as a list keyed by comment id

can you rewrite this blog post to remove every em-dash and replace each one with the most natural alternative — period, comma, semicolon, or parens — without changing any other wording? source is at /tmp/article.md, 750ish words, casual first-person voice so please keep contractions intact

ci is failing on the analyze job again. can you grab the failed-step log from `gh run view 8472918 --log-failed` and tell me what type of failure it is, which step blew up, and which file i should look at first? don't need a narrative, just the headline fields

i just landed 3 PRs in the feature-flag sprint (#372, #382, #383) and want to add a 'shipped' entry to docs/plans/current-plan.md. there's an existing entry at the top of the file with the right shape — can you draft one paragraph in the same style, using the PR numbers + squash hashes from `git log --oneline main~3..main`?

i'm adding a new 'Phase 13' section to ROADMAP.md and want a forward-looking intro paragraph for it. there's an existing phase intro a few sections up i'd like to match in voice and length — can you draft one paragraph in the same style from a fact list i'll give you (scope, anchors, planned sub-tracks)?

starting on a new area of the codebase — there's about 20 files in src/billing/ and i want a one-line description of each so i can pick the 3-4 i actually need to read. just enough so the directory listing tells me what each module's purpose is, don't need the full content

i'm picking up PR #196 that's been sitting for a week with 4 rounds of review on it — can you summarise where it stands? i need outstanding action items, who's waiting on whom, and what's been agreed so far, as three short sections. don't need the timeline, just the current state

before i tell the team this is fixed, can you check whether these three conclusions are actually backed by the CI log i'm looking at? i don't want to claim something the log doesn't actually say. the log is in ~/Downloads/run.log and i'll give you the three claims to verify against it

can you draft me a short two-sentence reply to send to a contributor who fixed a bug for us? first sentence praising the specific fix (an off-by-one in the pagination cursor), then one question asking them to add a regression test before merge, then a friendly 'thanks!' at the end. just give me the reply text
my test test_auth_refresh_flow is flaky — fails maybe 1 in 5 runs with a timeout on line 142. i've already added retries. can you dig in and find the root cause?
we're deciding between using kafka or kinesis for the new event pipeline. throughput is around 2k msgs/sec and we already use aws for everything else. which would you recommend?
can you review this PR? https://github.com/plg-tech/auth-service/pull/412 — it changes how we hash passwords and i want another set of eyes before we merge

i'm getting `TypeError: cannot read property 'map' of undefined` in our react dashboard on the /billing page, but only when the user has no invoices. can you trace why useInvoices() isn't returning an array?
write me a fastapi endpoint at POST /api/v2/users that validates the email format, checks uniqueness against postgres, and returns 201 with the new user id
refactor the models/billing.py file so the pricing logic is pulled out of the InvoiceGenerator class into a separate PricingEngine class. keep backward compat on the public methods

is there a security vulnerability in this jwt validation? it's verifying the signature but i'm not sure about the exp claim handling. here's the snippet: ```def verify(token): return jwt.decode(token, SECRET, algorithms=['HS256'])```
whats the difference between claude 4.6 and claude 4.7? i use claude code daily and wondering if i should upgrade
our nightly sync job failed last night with exit code 137. i've already checked the cloudwatch logs — OOM killed. can you tell me why memory spiked and suggest a fix?
i need to design a database schema for a multi-tenant saas where tenants can have nested orgs. should i use row-level security or separate schemas per tenant? walk me through the tradeoffs
what's the difference between a spirit and a liqueur? trying to follow a cocktail recipe and it just says 'spirit of choice'
if i invest 5k at 4.5% annual compounded monthly for 7 years, what's the final balance?
my dog keeps chewing the corner of the rug whenever i leave for work. she's 2yrs old, well-fed. is this anxiety or just boredom?
